title: APIM weak TLS + named value stored in cleartext
narrative: APIM accepts TLS 1.0 on the gateway AND stores backend credentials as cleartext named values. A downgrade-then-sniff attack on the gateway exposes traffic; reading the APIM config surface reveals backend secrets.

steps.action: Force TLS 1.0; sniff gateway traffic.
Read APIM named values; harvest backend creds.

data_at_risk: Every backend API the gateway fronts

title: Event Grid topic without authentication
narrative: An Event Grid topic accepts events from any source (no SAS, no AAD). An attacker publishes forged events that downstream consumers trust as legitimate — common for order-confirmation systems, payment events, and audit trails.

steps.action: POST a forged event to the topic endpoint.
Process the forged event as if it were real.

title: Service Bus SAS-only auth + key sprawl
narrative: Service Bus namespace uses only SAS policies (no Entra ID integration). The RootManageSharedAccessKey is shared across CI, apps, and developer laptops. A leaked key gives full send/receive on every queue and topic.

steps.action: Harvest RootManageSharedAccessKey from CI env var.
Read every queue; inject malicious messages.
steps.gain: Message bus poisoning + message content exfil.

title: Event Hub with Capture to public-access storage
narrative: Event Hub Capture writes every stream event to a storage account that has public blob access enabled. A downstream compliance leak: sensitive event data intended for a VNet-only datalake ends up readable over the internet.

steps.action: Capture writes to container with allowBlobPublicAccess=true.
Enumerate and download capture blobs; historical event stream exposed.

title: Logic Apps run history exposes secrets in trigger logs
narrative: Logic Apps run history records every input/output of every action. An action that calls an external API with a Bearer token stores the token in the run log. Any user with Logic App Reader can replay those tokens for follow-on access.

steps.action: Open Logic App run history; expand HTTP action inputs.
Replay token against target API.

title: Traffic Manager with HTTP (unencrypted) endpoint in pool
narrative: Traffic Manager profile includes at least one endpoint reachable over HTTP (port 80) not HTTPS. Failover to that endpoint drops TLS — users experience plaintext traffic to a 'secure' site during regional failures.

steps.action: Primary endpoint fails; failover activates HTTP-only endpoint.
steps.gain: User traffic routed to plaintext endpoint.

steps.action: Sniff or MITM user sessions.

title: Front Door origin pool without health probe
narrative: Front Door origin pool has no health probe configured. A failed origin silently receives traffic and returns 502s. During an incident, Front Door cannot route around the failure — availability SLA is meaningless.

steps.action: Origin returns 500s; Front Door continues to route.
Extended user-facing outage.

title: API Connection with embedded creds — shared across Logic Apps
narrative: An API Connection object stores credentials inline and is referenced by 20+ Logic Apps. Rotating the underlying credential means coordinating edits across all 20 workflows — so it never happens. One credential leak compromises the whole portfolio of integrations.

steps.action: Extract API Connection credential via ARM Reader.
Use credential; 20 Logic Apps look like legitimate callers.

title: Integration Account EDI content without encryption
narrative: B2B EDI messages (X12, EDIFACT) flow through an Integration Account without at-rest encryption beyond Microsoft defaults. Regulated industries (healthcare, financial trading) require CMK — audit finding plus liability.

steps.action: Auditor flags Integration Account without CMK.
Subpoena + no customer-controlled key means no plausible deniability.

title: Service Bus Relay without authentication
narrative: A WCF Service Bus Relay exposes an on-prem service to the internet without authentication. Anyone who discovers the relay URL reaches the backend service directly — bypassing corporate perimeter controls.

steps.action: Enumerate *.servicebus.windows.net for relay endpoints.
Exploit the backend app directly.

title: Notification Hub secret rotation gap
narrative: Azure Notification Hubs access keys haven't rotated in 2+ years. Mobile apps still embed the original key. A compromised key lets an attacker push arbitrary notifications to every registered device.

steps.action: Extract key from decompiled mobile app.
Push phishing notifications to every device.

title: Service Bus topic with over-privileged SAS
narrative: A Service Bus topic distributes events to dozens of subscribers. A single SAS with Manage + Send + Listen is used by every producer and consumer. Any compromise grants full control over the topic.

steps.action: Obtain the shared SAS from any one consumer.
Delete subscriptions or inject poisoned events.

title: Event Grid Domain with wildcard subscription filter
narrative: An Event Grid Domain has a subscription filter using wildcard * matching. A new event with attacker-controlled fields reaches every subscriber. Combined with a subscriber that trusts field content blindly, this becomes an injection vector.

steps.action: Publish event with crafted fields.
Interprets attacker-controlled field as command parameter.

title: Event Hub partition key predictable — message replay
narrative: Event Hub is used for audit trail ingestion. Partition keys are simple (user email). An attacker with any SAS can publish arbitrary events to any partition — forging 'audit' entries attributed to any user.

steps.action: Craft forged audit event with victim user's partition key.
Trusts the audit log as-is; investigation misdirected.

title: Logic App with OAuth connection to over-privileged SP
narrative: A Logic App uses an OAuth connection that was authorised with Global Admin consent. Every run of the Logic App executes with Global Admin scope — a scheduled trigger becomes a recurring privileged operation nobody audits.

steps.action: Consent to OAuth connection while signed in as Global Admin.
Edit workflow to exfil directory data; run uses GA token.
